title: General
description: General engineering backlog for cross-project tasks
summary: General engineering queue for hardware fixtures, small CAD jobs, gripper platforms, camera adapters, glove-to-gripper teleoperation, and tactile calibration hardware. These items are intentionally separate from Real-Robot Lab Infra and can support multiple projects when needed.
details: Purpose: keep small hardware design jobs out of paper-specific project cards while still making them visible and assignable. Most items can be progressed remotely through CAD, wiring/interface sketches, BOM choices, and print/manufacturing handoff.

Main platform gap: the lab lacks reusable low-DoF gripper and tactile-sensor test platforms. A simple two-finger FinRay/GelSight/PPTAC-style platform and a three-finger tactile gripper would cover many VLA/VTLA experiments better than relying only on dexterous hands.

Three-finger direction: the PSA proposal can use a three-finger gripper with fingertip tactile sensors; the Zhu-boss motor option refers to YinKeSi motors. Target demos include Jenga-style block extraction and dexterous-hand / gripper knife-cutting meat studies.

Two-finger direction: build a simple repeatable platform around FinRay fingers, GelSight-style tactile sensing, and PPTAC two-finger gripper ideas, then connect it to MANUS/Wuji glove retargeting and VLA/VTLA training data.
Sensor sourcing: do not buy new tactile sensors unless necessary. First ask Zheng Yuhang to get three Daimon tactile sensors for the three-finger gripper / calibration platform path.

Mounting direction: the Wuji/Franka camera flange is done; the next MANUS/Wuji glove camera adapter should follow a DexCap-style camera-mount and quick-release logic, keeping the observation camera stable across human collection and robot-side use while preserving field of view and cable strain relief.
Teleop direction: the Gello-end trigger can be replaced by an ACE Teleop-style wrist ring for wrist/intention input, while MANUS provides hand posture for retargeting.
Storage direction: repurpose the purchased 10T HDD as a small group NAS if the Taobao 7-day-return enclosure works; treat it as shared lab infrastructure rather than a UMI-only staging disk.
Procurement need: buy two USB 3.0 extension cables from Shopee for lab hardware routing; confirm whether direct group-card payment is allowed before checkout.

Procurement need: add a short camera screw item. User wording is six-inch short screw; before purchase, confirm whether this actually means the common camera/tripod 1/4-20 short screw rather than a 6-inch-long screw.
risks_decisions: Ownership risk: several items need a remote CAD owner plus a local hardware owner for printing, sensor pickup, and test fitting.
Sourcing decision: use Daimon tactile sensors through Zheng Yuhang first; avoid purchasing GelSight/PPTAC-like components until the available sensor inventory is confirmed.
Scope decision: start with low-DoF gripper platforms because they are easier to instrument, calibrate, and use for VLA/VTLA datasets than full dexterous-hand hardware.
Integration risk: camera mounts and wrist rings must avoid blocking hand motion, tactile contact surfaces, glove cables, and emergency access.
NAS risk: the Taobao enclosure must physically support the actual 10T HDD form factor/interface and provide stable power, permissions, backup, and LAN sharing before the return window closes.

Procurement risk: do not store group-card details in dashboard state; confirm Shopee direct-card purchase approval with the card holder / lab admin before ordering, otherwise use the approved reimbursement or procurement path.

Camera-screw procurement risk: camera mounts usually use 1/4-20 or 3/8-inch standards, while a literal six-inch screw would be unusually long. Confirm thread standard and usable shaft length before ordering.

timeline.target_note: Collect small gripper, mount, and tactile-platform jobs that support multiple robot projects.

asset_alt: General engineering backlog diagram for grippers, Jenga tooling, camera mounts, and calibration fixtures
asset_caption: General backlog: cross-project gripper, tactile, mount, wrist-ring, and calibration tasks.
